summary: Sudden hypotension post-epidural injection indicates potential systemic absorption due to admixed adrenaline; no delay rules out direct spinal effect and supports quicker hemodynamic changes instead. Adrenaline can lead to vasodilation or decreased peripheral resistance resulting in low BP quickly upon entering circulation from an unintended pathway during local block attempts. Agent1 concludes by highlighting how the quick symptom appearance strongly implies arterial access allowing widespread pharmacological impact swiftly impacting overall circulatory status through its known sympathomimetic actions before any significant intrathecal spread occurs which would take longer typically observed clinically without such acute presentation noted here immediately post-administration.
